notes: initial baseline (VPS under load: 2 long-running sims consuming ~970% CPU)

hypothesis: Integer-only distance comparison in inner loop
Skip ring iteration when search covers entire grid; flat scan contiguous SoA arrays
basis: T1: structural opportunity in grid traversal
notes: regression: ring early exit saves more than cell overhead costs

hypothesis: Fuse 4 output head loops into single 20-wide loop in CompactBrain::forward
basis: T1: structural opportunity in brain forward pass
notes: inconclusive: below 2% threshold; base_hidden fits L1 cache line, no reload savings

hypothesis: AVX2 SIMD 8-wide distance+wrap in receive_detailed_grid inner loop
notes: A/B VPS benchmark, paired analysis runs 5-7 (stable load), SNR 3.5. First KEEP.

hypothesis: v15 re-baseline (VPS pinned cores 6-11, RAYON_NUM_THREADS=6, znver3)
notes: re-baseline: 50 gens median 6.899s = 7.25 gps. Determinism baseline captured. All future A/B benchmarks use taskset -c 6-11.

hypothesis: Replace rem_euclid with wrap_coord in SignalGrid::rebuild (4 calls per signal)
notes: inconclusive: A/B SNR 0.42 (CoV 7%+7%), delta in noise. T4 micro-opt.

hypothesis: Split Prey into hot (~50B) and cold (~180B) structs for cache density
basis: T1: structural opportunity in Prey struct layout
notes: inconclusive: batch1 +3.4% SNR 0.52 (heavy load), batch2 -0.97% SNR ~0. 14 paired A/B runs, no signal above noise. T1 structural.

hypothesis: Per-cell SIMD padding with sentinels to eliminate scalar tail in receive_detailed_grid
notes: A/B VPS benchmark, paired analysis, SNR 3.22. Pad SoA cells to 8-wide with i16::MAX sentinels, remove scalar tail entirely. T0 profile-driven.

hypothesis: Pad CompactBrain hidden layer to SIMD width (bh=12 -> padded_bh=16) for scalar tail elimination
notes: regression: 6/7 pairs show E slower. Padding adds 33% more iterations + fast_tanh calls; for bh=12 LLVM auto-vectorizes efficiently without padding. T0 profile-driven.
